exemplar.title:
How do neonicotinoid insecticides affect honeybee populations and what implications does it cause to the agricultural ecosystems in North Dakota?
Subject: World Studies [Biology and Chemistry]

detailedExemplarData.title:
How do neonicotinoid insecticides affect honeybee populations and what implications does it cause to the agricultural ecosystems in North Dakota?
Subject: World Studies [Biology and Chemistry]

strengths: Title clearly states a geographically bounded focus
Research question is explicit and guides the investigation
Consistent use of headings and signposting maintains clarity
weaknesses: Methodology lacks detail on sourceâselection criteria and analytical techniques
No primary data collection or explanation of analytical procedures
Research question covers two outcomes, slightly broadening scope
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The topic and research question are communicated clearly (band 5â6), but the methodology description is only partially justified (band 3). Overall performance aligns with band 3â4, meriting 4 marks out of 6.

strengths: Broad range of recent, interdisciplinary sources relevant to chemistry, toxicology and ecology
Accurate explanations of chemical structures (IUPAC nomenclature) and ecological impacts
Compelling integration of economic valuation of pollination services
weaknesses: Occasional reliance on popular websites and duplicated bibliography entries
Minor citation omissions (e.g., crop reliance statement)
Terminology use is mostly accurate but lacks consistent depth
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Source selection and knowledge application sit solidly in the middle band (4/6), with mostly accurate terminology (band 3). The overall integration of interdisciplinary content justifies 4 marks out of 6.

strengths: Rigorous selection of peerâreviewed literature supporting interdisciplinary analysis
Coherent linkage from chemical polarity to bee neuroâtoxicity and economic outcomes
Critical acknowledgment of confounding factors and study limitations
weaknesses: Quantitative analysis is limited; lacks regional toxicity datasets
Interdisciplinary synthesis remains at surface level without deep counterâarguments
Conclusions concede multiple variables cannot be fully isolated
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Research quality meets the 7â9 band threshold, analysis is coherent but not incisive, and argumentation shows emerging critical insight. These collectively place the work at the lower end of band 7â9, warranting 7 marks out of 12.

strengths: Clear academic structure with title page, contents, introduction and conclusion
Consistent inâtext citations and overall referencing style
weaknesses: Table of contents formatting and indentation are inconsistent
Duplicated entries and broken URLs in the bibliography
Several figures lack formal captions or textual discussion
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The report demonstrates clear academic conventions, relevant visuals and largely consistent referencing, placing it firmly in band 3â4 for Presentation and meriting 3 marks out of 4.

strengths: Some minimal personal context in the introduction
Choice of interdisciplinary case study shows basic initiative
weaknesses: No RPPF or formal reflections on planning and challenges
Limited personal engagement beyond topic choice
No evidence of original data collection or future research proposals
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: With only minimal personal engagement and initiative and no reflective commentary, the work falls into band 1â2. A single mark reflects this limited level of Engagement.

strandSelections.reasoning: Headings create a logical progression from chemistry to ecology to economics, indicating structure.  Some evaluative comments (e.g., acknowledging confounding factors) show emerging critical insight, yet interdisciplinary synthesis remains surface-level and counter-arguments are lightly handled.

A single, alphabetised bibliography in MLA style is provided; in-text citations appear throughout.  Duplicated entries and occasional URL breaks indicate minor inconsistency, but the formatting is largely correct and complete, satisfying the upper band.

The student states an explicit, geographically bounded topic (impact of neonicotinoids on honey-bee populations and agricultural ecosystems in North Dakota) in both the title and the opening section. Throughout the essay, headings, sub-headings and signposting consistently maintain this focus and keep the reader oriented, demonstrating clarity and appropriateness.

A full academic apparatus is present: title page, word count, contents page, introduction, main body, conclusion and works cited.  Pagination and section numbering appear consistent in the extract, though some page numbers in the ToC are mistyped.  Overall structure is clear and coherent.

The essay explains chemical structures, LD50 ranges and ecological consequences, weaving supporting studies into the narrative.  Knowledge is generally accurate and goes beyond description, yet integration between chemistry and biology is occasional rather than seamless and some statements remain asserted, so the strand fits the middle band.
No RPPF extracts or explicit reflections are supplied with the submission, so there is no evidence on which to assess planning or evaluative thinking.

The student links chemical polarity to bee neuro-toxicity and then to economic outcomes, showing coherent chains of reasoning.  Still, quantitative depth is limited and the conclusion concedes that variables cannot be isolated, so a solid but not extensive level of support is demonstrated.

The research question is quoted verbatim, is sufficiently narrow (specific chemicals, single state) and clearly interdisciplinary.  Minor breadth remains (two distinct outcomes â honey-bees and whole agro-ecosystem) but it is still fully workable and guides the investigation effectively.

The majority of cited papers are recent peer-reviewed articles; they directly support the interdisciplinary angle (toxicology, agronomy, economics).  Some grey literature is included, but overall the research base is well chosen and relevant.

Choosing an interdisciplinary case study shows some initiative, yet there is no evidence of original data collection, novel methodology or future research ideas.  Initiative is therefore assessed as minimal.

The methodology is described under âWorld Studies Approach,â identifying Biology for effects and Chemistry for causes, and indicating secondary literature, statistical data and case-study comparison.  However, there is little detail on criteria for source selection, no primary data collection, and no explanation of analytical techniques.  This represents generally appropriate but only partially justified methods.

Roughly forty scholarly, recent and thematically pertinent sources are listed, covering chemistry, toxicology, ecology and economics.  While some duplication and a few popular websites appear, the bulk of material is relevant and academic, placing the work solidly in the âmostly relevant and appropriateâ band.
The introduction briefly states personal interest in North Dakotaâs unique conditions, but this is limited.  Without reflective commentary, engagement appears minimal rather than absent.

Three figures are included, each given a caption and source.  While resolution is low and analytical discussion of visuals is minimal, the images are relevant (state map, chemical structure, environmental pathway diagram) and correctly labelled, meeting the âwell-chosen, correctly labelledâ descriptor.

Key terms such as nAChRs, LD50, polarity, CCD and ecosystem services are used correctly and repeatedly.  Depth is adequate but not extensive; a few chemical formulae are simplified and minor typographical errors occur.  Terminology is therefore âmostly accurateâ rather than consistently sophisticated.

annotations.comment: The subsection heading is clear and logically placed, supporting a coherent structure for the reader.
The multi-part figure layout spans a large area with reduced clarity; consider splitting into two focused figures.
Avoid embedding HTML underlines in headings; use native formatting styles to maintain consistency.
The economic impact discussion is compelling, with global pollination valuation well integrated.
The recommendation to chemically modify neonicotinoids is broad; propose specific pathways or policy measures to make the conclusion more actionable.
The bibliography on the final page shows a good range of recent, peer-reviewed sources that support interdisciplinary depth.
The title precisely communicates a clear, geographically bounded focus on neonicotinoids and honeybee impacts in North Dakota, demonstrating excellent topic identification.
The research question introduces two outcomes (bee populations and ecosystems) which could be split or further specified to enhance focus and ensure manageable scope.
Including the word count at the start meets formatting requirements and shows attention to IB guidelines.
Several page references in the ToC include inline math notation (e.g., âPage 6â), which disrupts readability; replace with plain text.
The ToC lists all sections but shows inconsistent indentation and formatting for sub-headings; standardize list formatting for clarity.
The introduction paragraph is very dense and covers multiple historical phases in one block; break into smaller paragraphs to improve flow and reader engagement.
The page number appears as â\overline{3}â likely due to OCR; verify pagination style to maintain academic consistency.
The case-study justification for North Dakota is brief; expand on unique ecological or agricultural factors that make this location ideal.
Figure is embedded without a formal caption or reference in text; include a numbered caption and discuss its relevance in the narrative.
The methodology description under âWorld Studies Approachâ lacks detail on specific analytical techniques and criteria for source selection.
The repeated phrasing of the research question could be streamlined; consider paraphrasing here for smoother flow.
You note that only ~5% of neonicotinoids are absorbed by plants; adding the original studyâs statistical year and context would strengthen credibility.
The opening of this section contains a long sentence that would benefit from being split for improved readability.
Excellent explanation of chemical derivation and accurate use of IUPAC nomenclature, showcasing strong subject knowledge.
The structural figure is included without a descriptive caption or reference; embed a caption and label it as Figure 2 or 3.
The discussion of nAChR selectivity is detailed and accurate, demonstrating deep understanding of receptor-binding differences.
Initial figure reference is shown in square brackets; formal caption formatting and numbering should replace this placeholder.
Inline units such as â14.6 Î¼g/beeâ should be formatted using LaTeX, e.g. $$14.6~\mu g/bee$$, to meet academic presentation standards.
While subgroup toxicity is described, adding North Dakotaâspecific water toxicity data would deepen the regional relevance.
The narrative on colony decline lacks quantitative trend analysis; include a graph or dataset to support claims of population change.
Acknowledging confounding factors such as climate and disease demonstrates strong critical insight into limitations.
Linking Colony Collapse Disorder to neonicotinoids is strong, but specify sample sizes and experimental conditions to support the CCD claims.
The statement that major North Dakota crops do not rely on pollinators is correct but needs citation for confirmation.
The section header effectively signals a new focus on wild ecosystems, supporting logical flow.
Discussion of wild ecosystem services would benefit from a concrete case example of pollinator-driven restoration near farms.
Several entries in the bibliography are duplicated (e.g., Akter et al. appears twice); remove duplicates and verify alphabetical order.
The Britannica citation lacks publication date and proper MLA formatting; include date and publisher details.
URLs in the bibliography are broken across lines and not hyperlinked; consolidate each URL on one line and use a consistent style.
